title: Rich Russian Potatoes Gratin
description: A creamy, buttery potato gratin with a molten top, flavored with herbs and a touch of mustard, perfect for a hearty, sugar-free Russian-inspired meal.

ingredients.name: beurre-monte
ingredients.notes: butter and olive oil melted together

instructions: Wash, peel, and slice the potatoes into 1/4-inch thick rounds.
In a small saucepan, melt the beurre-monte over medium heat until it becomes slightly browned.
Add the minced garlic to the beurre-monte and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Stir in the heavy cream, fresh thyme, salt, pepper, and lemon juice, and bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and fold in the grated gruyere and parmesan cheeses until the sauce is smooth.
Grease a 9x13-inch baking dish with a thin layer of beurre-monte.
Arrange a layer of potato slices in the dish, pour a little of the cheese sauce over the top, and repeat until all potatoes are used up.
Pour the remaining sauce over the final layer of potatoes and sprinkle additional cheese if desired.
Bake uncovered for 45 minutes, or until the potatoes are tender and the top is golden and bubbly.
Remove the gratin from the oven, let it rest for 10 minutes, and serve hot.
